Title: The basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor BIS2 is essential for monoterpenoid indole alkaloid production in the medicinal plant Catharanthus roseus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) clade IVa transcription factor with ACT domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: internal phloem-associated parenchyma (IPAP)-associated tissues; suspension cells; petal limbs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence compounds; includes anticancer alkaloids vinblastine and vincristine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator of the iridoid branch supplying MIA biosynthesis; overexpression increases iridoid gene expression and accumulation of loganic acid, secologanin, strictosidine, ajmalicine, serpentine and tabersonine, whereas RNAi silencing abolishes jasmonate-induced iridoid gene induction and subsequent MIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of the JA-inducible iridoid precursor pathway required for MIA production in C. roseus suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of iridoid biosynthesis and upstream MEP precursor supply
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GES; G8O; 8HGO; IS; 7DLGT; 7DLH; IO; DXR; BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple iridoid pathway promoters and promotes transcript accumulation of MEP/iridoid genes; does not activate most downstream ORCA-regulated MIA genes such as SLS, LAMT, SGD and TDC
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive amplification loop: BIS2 expression is induced by BIS1 overexpression and by BIS2 overexpression itself
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimers and heterodimers with BIS1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in MP183L suspension cells; RNAi silencing in suspension cells; transient ectopic expression in petal limbs; promoter transactivation assays in tobacco protoplasts and C. roseus cells; split-LUC; yeast two-hybrid; qRT-PCR/RNA blot; targeted metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA-responsive in suspension cells, hairy roots and seedlings; preferentially expressed in internal phloem-associated parenchyma cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-responsive in suspension cells, hairy roots and seedlings; enriched in IPAP-containing leaf and stem tissues; highly expressed after ectopic overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment; geraniol feeding for metabolite detection in silenced lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; endogenous iridoid gene promoters in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome mining/co-expression analysis using published RNA-seq/FPKM datasets (Gongora-Castillo et al. 2012; Van Moerkercke et al. 2013; Miettinen et al. 2014); no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to boost iridoid precursor supply and MIA production in medicinal C. roseus; likely best combined with regulators or enzymes controlling downstream MIA branches because BIS2 alone does not induce most downstream MIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS2 is a JA-responsive clade IVa bHLH TF coexpressed with MEP and iridoid pathway genes. 2. BIS2 activates promoters of multiple iridoid biosynthetic genes and forms homo- and heterodimers with BIS1. 3. BIS2 overexpression elevates seco-iridoid and MIA accumulation, whereas BIS2 silencing abolishes JA-induced iridoid gene expression and MIA production. 4. BIS2 participates in a positive amplification loop because BIS1 and BIS2 enhance BIS2 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BIS2 did not induce triterpenoid genes, MVK or CRTISO, and had little or no effect on most downstream ORCA-regulated MIA genes. Longer BIS2 promoter fragments could not be cloned; direct promoter binding was not proven.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cv. Sandstorm; hairy roots; petal limbs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) clade IVa transcription factor with ACT domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IPAP-associated tissues; hairy roots; suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence compounds; includes anticancer alkaloids vinblastine and vincristine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of JA-inducible iridoid biosynthesis and MIA precursor supply; in this paper BIS1 induces BIS2 expression and forms homo-/heterodimers with BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-regulator of the iridoid branch, but BIS2 is shown here to be the essential component for full JA-induced MIA production
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates iridoid pathway genes and upregulates BIS2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Promotes BIS2 amplification loop; no evidence here for BIS1 autoregulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimers and heterodimers with BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter transactivation assays; split-LUC; yeast two-hybrid; qRT-PCR of BIS2 induction in BIS1-overexpressing tissues; reference to prior stable overexpression/silencing studies
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA-responsive; enriched in IPAP tissues (from prior and current comparative data)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with iridoid genes; overexpression in hairy roots/petals induces BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome co-expression datasets referenced from prior studies; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering regulator for enhancing iridoid precursor formation, potentially combined with BIS2 or downstream MIA regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 is closely related to BIS2 and activates the same iridoid target promoters. 2. BIS1 forms homo- and heterodimers with BIS2. 3. BIS1 overexpression induces BIS2 transcript accumulation, indicating a positive amplification role. 4. Despite BIS1 induction, BIS2 silencing abolishes JA-induced MIA accumulation, showing BIS1 alone is insufficient in this context.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BIS1 is mainly contextual/supporting in this paper rather than the principal newly characterized gene.
